name: Request
summary: Request objects allows you to send HTTP request to HTTP servers.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: Note: If no reference is kept to the Sketchup::Http::Request, it can be garbage collected, making the download silently fail. This is especially noticeable for larger downloads that takes longer time. Note: Do not #cancel the request in the response callback. The default port is 80, to use a different port define it in the URL when creating a new Sketchup::Http::Request. The #method parameter accepts any custom HTTP method or one of the following: GET POST PUT DELETE HEAD OPTIONS
constructors.remarks: Note: If no reference is kept to the Sketchup::Http::Request, it can be garbage collected, making the download silently fail. This is especially noticeable for larger downloads that takes longer time. Note: Do not #cancel the request in the response callback. The default port is 80, to use a different port define it in the URL when creating a new Sketchup::Http::Request. The #method parameter accepts any custom HTTP method or one of the following: GET POST PUT DELETE HEAD OPTIONS

methods.name: cancel
methods.summary: Note: Do not #cancel the request in the response callback.
methods.remarks: Note: Do not #cancel the request in the response callback. Cancels the request.

methods.name: set_download_progress_callback
methods.summary: Note: total is -1 if the server doesn't specify a file size in the response header.
methods.remarks: Note: total is -1 if the server doesn't specify a file size in the response header. Adds a download progress callback block that will get called everytime we have received data from the server until the download finishes.

methods.name: set_upload_progress_callback
methods.summary: Adds a upload progress callback block that will get called everytime we have uploaded data to the server until the upload finishes.
methods.remarks: Adds a upload progress callback block that will get called everytime we have uploaded data to the server until the upload finishes.

methods.name: start
methods.summary: Note: Do not #cancel the request in the response callback.
methods.remarks: Note: Do not #cancel the request in the response callback. Starts the request with optionally a response callback block.

methods.name: status
methods.remarks: Returns the internal status code. It can be one of the following: Sketchup::Http::STATUS_UNKNOWN Sketchup::Http::STATUS_SUCCESS Sketchup::Http::STATUS_PENDING Sketchup::Http::STATUS_CANCELED Sketchup::Http::STATUS_FAILED

methods.name: url
methods.summary: Returns a copy of the Request's URL.
methods.remarks: Returns a copy of the Request's URL.

properties.name: body
properties.summary: Gets the HTTP body that is going to be used when sending the request.
properties.remarks: Gets the HTTP body that is going to be used when sending the request.

properties.name: headers
properties.summary: Returns the HTTP headers that are going to be used when sending the request.
properties.remarks: Returns the HTTP headers that are going to be used when sending the request.

properties.name: method
properties.summary: Returns the HTTP method that is going to be used when sending the request.
properties.remarks: Returns the HTTP method that is going to be used when sending the request.
